content.subtitle: Uses
items: When we don't know who does/did something
When it's obvious who does/did something
When it's not important who does/did something

content.subtitle: Examples
items: Three journalists were detained during the protests that swept through the capital last weekend.
Having been ratified in 1948, the Universal Declaration of Human Rights continues to shape international law.
The championship trophy was lifted at the closing ceremony by the tournament's youngest-ever captain.
This deep-sea drilling technique was pioneered during the 1960s by marine geologists working off the coast of California.
We were astonished by the scale of the destruction left behind by the overnight storm.
All passengers are required to present valid identification before boarding the international flight.

content.subtitle: Form
items: noun + verb in passive form + infinitive / perfect infinitive
There + verb in passive form + infinitive / perfect infinitive
It + verb in passive form + that clause

content.subtitle: Examples
items: The global population is expected to reach approximately ten billion by the middle of this century.
There are reported to have been unusually high levels of air pollution across the northern region this winter.
It is believed that the discovery of the ancient tomb will shed new light on early civilisation in the region.

content.subtitle: Form
items: Some verbs (bring, buy, get, give, leave, lend, make, offer, owe, pass, pay, promise, refuse, send, show, take, teach, tell, write) can take two objects.

content.subtitle: Example
items: Active: The panel offered the young scientist a prestigious grant.
Passive (indirect object as subject): The young scientist was offered a prestigious grant (by the panel).
Passive (direct object as subject): A prestigious grant was offered to the young scientist (by the panel).

content.subtitle: Watch out!
items: With explain and suggest, the preposition cannot be omitted. They cannot be used with indirect object as subject.

content.subtitle: Tenses to avoid
content.description: The passive is not normally used with present perfect continuous, past perfect continuous, future continuous or future perfect continuous.

content.subtitle: Examples
items: ✓ Restoration work on the cathedral roof will be in progress throughout the winter months.
✓ By the time she completes her residency, Elena will have been in training as a cardiologist for six years.
✓ Rare artefacts from the ancient Egyptian collection have been on display in the east wing for several weeks.
✓ By the time the committee reached a verdict, the proposal had been under consideration for over a year.
✓ The new coastal motorway has been under construction for nearly two years now.

content.subtitle: Uses
items: Actions we arrange for other people to do for us
Things we experience (usually negative and not intended)

content.subtitle: Examples
items: Have you managed to get your bicycle repaired before the race?
I later found out that James had his briefcase stolen on the train.

content.subtitle: Watch out!
items: Get is more informal than have.
Can use need, want, would like: I'd like those samples analysed before the meeting.
Get sth done can also mean 'finish doing something': Let's leave as soon as I've got the presentation prepared.

sections.title: Causative: get sb to do / have sb do

content.subtitle: Form
items: Actions we make somebody/something do for us

content.subtitle: Example
items: Did you get Marcus to translate the whole report into French?

content.subtitle: Form
items: Actions we make somebody/something start doing

content.subtitle: Example
items: Don't worry. We'll soon have the new system running smoothly.

sections.title: A: Correct the Passive Form
sections.instructions: If the verb in bold is correct, put a tick. If it is incorrect, rewrite it correctly.

items.sentence: Could you confirm whether the planning application **was been approved** by the local council?
The examination timetable **was finally announced** on the university's official website on Monday.
**Has** your accommodation at the research institute next semester **confirmed** by the housing office?
Every scientist who **was been invited** to the symposium submitted a written paper in advance.
Penicillin, the first true antibiotic, **invented** in 1928 by Scottish bacteriologist Alexander Fleming.
The controversial bill **has debated** in the chamber last Tuesday, but no vote was taken.
By the time the delegates reach the summit, all the equipment **should have been unloaded** from the transport vehicles.
The burial chamber **was be discovered** by a team of archaeologists during excavations carried out in 2003.
The annual charity auction **had been organised** well in advance, yet a few donated items went uncatalogued.
After **be examined** thoroughly by customs officers, the crates were finally cleared for entry.
We arrived at the conference venue and **were directing** to the registration desk by a member of staff.
The restored Victorian bridge **is to be inaugurated** by the mayor at a public ceremony next Saturday.

sections.title: B: Passive Verb Forms
sections.instructions: Write a verb from the box in the correct passive form in each gap.

items.sentence: The missing keys ……………………. under a stack of folders on the office table just this morning.
At the new botanical garden, the first special exhibition next spring ……………………… will focus on rare endangered orchids.
Several searching questions …………………………… in the boardroom about the company's environmental record.
We are all really excited about ………………………… an exclusive behind-the-scenes tour of the observatory.
Consider that when these poems ……………………… , electricity had not yet been widely distributed to the public.
Once the ceremonial torch ………………………… , the athletes will begin their parade around the stadium.
Before ……………………………… onto the factory floor, each visitor had to complete a health and safety briefing.
He had always dreamed of ………………………… at the harbour by someone who truly understood his work.
It ………………………………… abundantly plain to the board of directors that the merger negotiations would be suspended.
Professor Chen says she has always hoped …………………………… as one of the pioneers of modern climate science.

sections.title: C: Impersonal Passive
sections.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: The decline of coral reefs is thought **to be caused / is caused** by a combination of rising ocean temperatures and chemical runoff.
There **are to be estimated / are estimated** to be over two million insect species yet to be formally classified by science.
It **is to be reported / has been reported** that a major cyberattack has compromised several government databases.
The prime suspect is believed **to use / to have used** a forged identity document to cross the border undetected.
It **is to be calculated / is calculated** that around 40% of the world's food supply is wasted before it reaches consumers.
More than 200 households **are being understood / are understood** to have been evacuated following the chemical spill.
It is considered disrespectful in many traditions **to be pointed / to point** at religious monuments or sacred objects.
There **mean / were meant** to have been fifteen delegates at the summit, not just five!
**It / There** has been suggested that all new public buildings should be designed to meet net-zero energy standards.
Your order is guaranteed **will be delivered / to be delivered** in a climate-controlled vehicle within five working days.

sections.title: D: Error Correction (Colosseum Text)
sections.instructions: Each sentence contains one verb in the wrong form. Underline the incorrect word or phrase and rewrite it correctly.

items.sentence: The Colosseum, situated at the heart of ancient Rome, **has called** one of the most remarkable feats of engineering ever achieved in the ancient world.
The site on which the amphitheatre stands **sold** to the imperial authorities following the great fire that devastated the city in 64 AD.
...designed to host grand public spectacles, **was named** the Flavian Amphitheatre after the ruling dynasty that commissioned its construction...
...the underground network, which **has later renamed** the hypogeum after excavations revealed its complex system of tunnels and trapdoors.
...ensuring that spectators could **been transported** efficiently to their seats by means of a sophisticated system of numbered entrances.
The upper tiers and the external arcade... **have been completed** before the inaugural games were staged under Emperor Titus in 80 AD.
...a retractable canvas awning known as the velarium **has been incorporated** into the original design, provided that sufficient naval personnel could be recruited to manage it.

sections.title: E: Avoiding the Passive
sections.instructions: Complete the sentences describing each situation using the phrase given.

items.sentence: By the time she earns her medical licence, ......
items.answer: she will have been in training as a surgeon for five years.

items.sentence: The proposal to extend the city's cycling infrastructure ......
items.answer: has been under consideration for several months.

items.sentence: Talks between the union and company management ......
items.answer: have been in progress for more than three weeks.

items.sentence: The sculptor's latest bronze works ......
items.answer: will be on display at the main hall this weekend.

items.sentence: The long-awaited bypass around the village centre ......
items.answer: appears to have been under construction for far too long.

sections.title: F: Causative Error Correction
sections.instructions: If the word or phrase in bold is correct, put a tick. If it is incorrect, rewrite it correctly.

items.sentence: We ended up having it **replaced** twice!
Well, we got **delivered it** to the house on the morning of the installation.
We decided to have it **check** by a licensed builder straight away.
Oh, I didn't know you'd **had them do** that.
We had my neighbour **to take** a look at the foundations, just to be safe.
items.answer: had my neighbour take a look

items.sentence: Did you have the contractor **to come** to assess the extent of the structural damage?
We got my cousin **to working** on the garden landscaping in the meantime.
I bet he soon had **running it** on schedule.
...we still had to get the contractor **come** back out to address the drainage issue in the end...
You should get them **to offer** you a partial refund for all the inconvenience.

sections.title: G: Causative Structures
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence using the noun in bold and an appropriate causative form of the verb in bold.

items.sentence: If there is anything you do not understand about the assignment, ...... (your tutor / EXPLAIN) it to you once more before the seminar.
...hearing the old folk song on the radio soon ...... (me / FEEL) surprisingly nostalgic.
Don't attempt to rewire the kitchen yourself — ...... (a professional / DO) it safely.
...... (our equipment / DAMAGE) during the storm was a serious setback that delayed the entire fieldwork project.
You will never build real confidence in the subject if you constantly ...... (others / HELP) you with every single problem.
...the street performer soon ...... (us / LAUGH) at his extraordinary juggling routine.
Don't assume ...... (me / DO) your share of the group project as well!
Have you considered ...... (your shutters / PAINT) a darker shade to complement the new exterior tiles?
I ...... (Tom / SHOW) me the correct technique, but I still struggled to reproduce it independently.
Have you seen the documentary about celebrities ...... (their pictures / TAKE) without permission by press photographers?

sections.title: H: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Use between three and eight words.

items.sentence:
The border officials made us wait in the customs hall for nearly half an hour! **KEPT**
...... in the customs hall for nearly half an hour!

Although people regard Professor Okonkwo as an authority on the subject, she still attends every conference to learn. **DESPITE**
Despite ...... on the subject, Professor Okonkwo still attends every conference to learn.

We have received information that the minister is planning a surprise visit to the factory. **REPORTED**
The minister ...... a surprise visit to the factory.

The head coach offered all the junior athletes additional revision materials. **PROVIDED**
All the junior athletes ...... the head coach.
items.answer: were provided with additional revision materials by

items.sentence:
I really feel that somebody ought to go through these safety procedures with me before the drill. **GET**
I really think I ought to get ...... me before the drill.
items.answer: someone to go through these safety procedures with

items.sentence:
Builders have been working on the new sports stadium for much longer than the council originally promised. **UNDER**
The new sports stadium ...... for much longer than the council originally promised.

Do you know why the shift supervisor made Ellen work through her lunch break? **MADE**
Do you know why ...... through her lunch break by the shift supervisor?

Call the front desk and ask the receptionist to forward all enquiries to the relevant department. **HAVE**
Call the front desk and ...... all enquiries to the relevant department.
The moment the stand-up comedian took the microphone, she soon ...... with her sharp observations.
The government must ...... firm measures to tackle the growing housing shortage.
options: D be seen to be taking

items.sentence: The project manager asked me to let you know that she would like ...... by the end of business today.
options: D to have sent the reports

items.sentence: Switching to a plant-based diet ...... , though I was initially rather sceptical.
There ...... more than 400 species of shark currently inhabiting the world's oceans.
options: B have been said to be

items.sentence: ...she claims she ...... while sightseeing in the old quarter of the city.
options: B got her passport to be stolen
C had her passport be stolen

items.sentence: Why didn't you ...... you move all those boxes when you shifted into the new apartment?
Average global temperatures ...... by at least 1.5 degrees Celsius before the end of this century.
It is impressive that this entire bridge ...... without disturbing the surrounding wetland ecosystem.
As soon as Dad ...... , we can set off for the airport.
options: B has got the car started
C will have the car starting
D gets the car to start

sections.title: J: Open Cloze (Electric Vehicles)
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Imagine (1) ...... able to travel hundreds of kilometres without producing a single gram of direct exhaust emissions.
Electric vehicles (2) ...... increasingly regarded as one of the most promising solutions to urban air pollution and climate change.
Battery technology has long been (3) ...... development, with early research into lithium-ion cells dating back to the 1970s.
In a conventional combustion engine, fuel (4) ...... ignited inside a cylinder to produce the mechanical force that drives the vehicle.
...a software update is transmitted wirelessly along (5) ...... a detailed performance report, allowing the car to improve without visiting a garage.
Each vehicle, which first has to (6) ...... certified by the relevant safety authority, undergoes rigorous testing before reaching the consumer market.
It has (7) ...... estimated that widespread adoption of electric vehicles could cut transport-related carbon emissions by up to 50% over the next two decades.
Despite this potential, the public charging infrastructure (8) ...... not yet been developed uniformly across rural and remote regions.
Although engineers (9) ...... successfully had battery packs deliver ranges exceeding 700 kilometres, achieving this consistently at an affordable price remains a significant challenge.
...and complete integration into public transport networks may (10) ...... several years away from full realisation.
